message:
feat(frankenterm-core): wire DECOM origin mode + wide-char support + fixtures (bd-lff4p.1.14)

- Add unicode-width dependency to frankenterm-core for Cell::display_width
- Wire wide-char (CJK/emoji) width handling in conformance harness apply_print
- Add decom_vpa_in_region fixture testing VPA with origin mode in scroll region
- Fix UTF-8 fixture expected values for wide-char display widths
- Include other agents' concurrent changes (snapshots, canvas, web renderer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","title":"Bench: web renderer frame-time harness + regression thresholds","description":"Goal
- Build a repeatable web performance harness for FrankenTerm that supports the Extreme Optimization loop.

Metrics (minimum)
- Frame time histogram (p50/p95/p99) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Time-to-first-frame and time-to-interactive.
- Patch statistics: bytes per frame, dirty-span counts, draw-call counts.
- Memory usage (JS + WASM) steady-state and after stress (scrollback/resize).
- GPU timings when available (best-effort).

Outputs
- Machine-readable JSON for CI gating and local profiling.
- Optional JSONL stream for per-frame/per-event diagnostics (run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR).

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline runs are stable enough to detect regressions (document expected variance + thresholds).
- Harness can be driven by deterministic traces (record/replay) so profiles are comparable.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","title":"Implement: scrollback virtualization + smooth wheel/trackpad scrolling","description":"Goal
- Make scrollback feel fast and modern even with huge histories.

Scope
- Only render the visible viewport + small overscan; never iterate the whole scrollback each frame.
- Smooth wheel/trackpad scrolling with coalescing.
- Optional inertial scrolling for touch devices.

Testing
- Perf harness: 100k-line scrollback stays interactive; frame-time p95 stays within budget.
- JSONL logs include scrollback size, viewport range, and render cost.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","title":"Implement: in-terminal search (scrollback search + highlight + next/prev)","description":"Goal
- Provide xterm.js-class search UX: find text in scrollback, highlight matches, and jump next/prev.

Scope
- Search should respect Unicode normalization and be fast on large scrollback.
- Highlighting must be incremental (avoid full redraw).

Testing

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","title":"Implement: web input capture layer (keyboard/mouse/touch)","description":"Goal
- Capture browser input events and feed them into the ftui/FrankenTerm world with minimal latency and deterministic semantics.

Requirements
- Keyboard
  - consistent modifier semantics across browsers/OS
  - repeat handling
  - special keys and function keys
  - composition events integrate with IME plan (bd-lff4p.2.9)
- Mouse
  - click/drag/wheel with correct cell coordinates (respects DPR + font metrics)
  - selection gestures
- Touch
  - tap, long-press, scroll; optional pinch zoom

Testing + Logging
- Unit tests for key mapping tables and edge cases (Ctrl/Alt/Meta/Super).
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck modifiers; idempotent focus loss).
- E2E scripts emit JSONL: event counts, encoded bytes (when relevant), and any mapping fallbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic event encoding suitable for record/replay.
- No key ghosting; no stuck modifiers; focus changes handled correctly.
